message:
perf(transport): rewrite random load balancer to single-pass reservoir sampling

Three improvements to random endpoint selection in the LoadBalancer:

1. Early-return in select(): check for Random strategy before
   materializing the SmallVec of available endpoints, avoiding a
   per-call allocation+filter that was unnecessary for random selection.

2. Replace two-pass algorithm (count healthy, then index-select) with
   single-pass reservoir sampling in select_random_single_without_materializing().
   The old approach scanned endpoints twice and had a race-condition fallback
   where concurrent health transitions between passes could miss the target
   index. Reservoir sampling (Vitter's Algorithm R) selects uniformly in
   one pass with no fallback needed.

3. Add test_load_balancer_random_select_single_is_uniform_over_healthy()
   that verifies uniform distribution: 3000 draws over 3 healthy endpoints
   (with 2 unhealthy mixed in) must each land within 900-1100 range.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
